findings.problem: The core Peter-versus-Noreen confrontation is restated three times in near-identical debt/obligation terms, so scene-level leverage changes too slowly and the chapter's middle reads as reiteration instead of escalation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure the confrontations at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:363 so each beat introduces a distinct coercive mechanism (hospital access, household resource control, and personal boundary breach) with a new consequence, rather than re-arguing the same cost ledger premise.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, on reread of the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:363, each confrontation turns on a different leverage point and produces a different immediate outcome; fail if two or more exchanges still hinge on the same cost/obligation argument.

findings.problem: Narration repeatedly uses clipped fragments (e.g., stand-alone noun phrases) as pressure shorthand, which violates the constitution's anti-elliptical-clipping rule and weakens syntactic control at key emotional beats.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:159, replace clipped narrative fragments with grammatically complete sentences that keep the same sensory detail and pace.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:9, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:101, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 contains explicit subject-plus-finite-verb sentence structure (outside quoted lists/dialogue); fail if stand-alone clipped fragments remain in those narrative lines.

findings.problem: Peter's dialogue over-literalizes his style-bible lexical signature (records/cost/obligation/accounting) so persistently that voice precision drifts into mechanical repetition and flattens scene unpredictability.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Peter's lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:121, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:231, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:367 by retaining some formal control diction but adding non-ledger pressure language (status fear, custody anxiety, social image, bodily disgust) so his control strategy varies by moment.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if each revised Peter exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:115, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:213, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:367 includes at least one line of control language that is not financial/accounting-coded and still sounds period-authentic; fail if all three exchanges remain dominated by bookkeeping diction alone.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same 'as if' comparison scaffold more than ten times, creating a detectable sentence tic and flattening prose rhythm where sharper direct perception would carry more force.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:49, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:103, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:161, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:207, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:371, replace repeated 'as if' comparisons with direct action, physical sensation, or concrete social observation in Noreen-close POV.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter retains no more than two 'as if' constructions total in the cited cluster and each removed instance is replaced with concrete sensory or behavioral detail visible on the page; fail if the same comparison scaffold still appears three or more times in those revised beats.

summary: Chapter 15 cleanly tracks consequence and remains consistent with continuity and canonical past-tense narration, but unresolved medium-plus issues remain: repeated confrontation architecture, clipped narrative syntax, mechanical over-reuse of Peter's accounting register, and repeated 'as if' scaffolding. No canonical continuity contradiction was found, and no intimacy fade-to-black event is present in this chapter.
